root_text.an2.64:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ pabbajitasukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.65:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ nekkhammasukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.66:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ nirupadhisukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.67:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ anāsavasukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.68:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ nirāmisaṁ sukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.69:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ ariyasukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.70:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ cetasikaṁ sukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.71:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ nippītikaṁ sukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.72:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ upekkhāsukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.73:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ samādhisukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.74:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ nippītikārammaṇaṁ sukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.75:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ upekkhārammaṇaṁ sukhan”ti.
root_text.an2.76:1.5: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ sukhānaṁ yadidaṁ arūpārammaṇaṁ sukhan”ti.

translation_text.an2.64:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.64:1.3: The happiness of laypeople, and the happiness of renunciates.
translation_text.an2.64:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.64:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is the happiness of renunciates.”
translation_text.an2.65:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.65:1.3: Sensual happiness and the happiness of renunciation.
translation_text.an2.65:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.65:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is the happiness of renunciation.”
translation_text.an2.66:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.66:1.3: The happiness of attachments, and the happiness of no attachments.
translation_text.an2.66:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.66:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is the happiness of no attachments.”
translation_text.an2.67:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.67:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.67:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is the happiness of no defilements.”
translation_text.an2.68:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.68:1.3: Happiness of the flesh and happiness not of the flesh.
translation_text.an2.68:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.68:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is happiness not of the flesh.”
translation_text.an2.69:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.69:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.69:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is noble happiness.”
translation_text.an2.70:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.70:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.70:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is mental happiness.”
translation_text.an2.71:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.71:1.3: Happiness with rapture and happiness free of rapture.
translation_text.an2.71:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.71:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is happiness free of rapture.”
translation_text.an2.72:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.72:1.3: Happiness with pleasure and happiness with equanimity.
translation_text.an2.72:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.72:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is happiness with equanimity.”
translation_text.an2.73:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.73:1.3: The happiness of immersion and the happiness without immersion.
translation_text.an2.73:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.73:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is the happiness of immersion.”
translation_text.an2.74:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.74:1.3: Happiness that relies on rapture and happiness that relies on freedom from rapture.
translation_text.an2.74:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.74:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is happiness that relies on freedom from rapture.”
translation_text.an2.75:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.75:1.3: Happiness that relies on pleasure and happiness that relies on equanimity.
translation_text.an2.75:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.75:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is happiness that relies on equanimity.”
translation_text.an2.76:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.76:1.3: Happiness that relies on form and happiness that relies on the formless.
translation_text.an2.76:1.4: These are the two kinds of happiness.
translation_text.an2.76:1.5: The better of these two kinds of happiness is happiness that relies on the formless.”
